Interface PassiveSkill

All Superinterfaces:
Describable
All Known Implementing Classes:
AquaMaiden, ArmorShatter, Berserk, BreachingShot, DeathRailway, Enlightenment, FireResistance, HomeMadeCannon, Kingslayer, LongReach, PurifyingAura, Raze, StructuralRepair
public interface PassiveSkill extends Describable
Represents an automatic ability or trait possessed by a unit. Passive skills do not require an active player command; instead, they automatically trigger their effects when specific game phases or conditions are met.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final GameManager
    gm
    A globally accessible, static reference to the GameManager singleton.

    Identifies the specific timing or event trigger required for this passive skill to activate.

    Executes the core logic and effects of the passive skill.

    getDescription, getName

  • Field Details

    • gm

      static final GameManager gm
      A globally accessible, static reference to the GameManager singleton. Provided as a convenience so implementing skills can easily read the board state, check active phases, or print logs without needing to repeatedly call getInstance().

  • Method Details

    • getPassiveType

      PassiveType getPassiveType()
      Identifies the specific timing or event trigger required for this passive skill to activate.
      Returns:
      The PassiveType enum representing the trigger condition (e.g., Phase Start, On Death).

    • performPassive

      void performPassive(BaseUnit unit)
      Executes the core logic and effects of the passive skill. This method is automatically called by the BaseUnit when the game reaches the appropriate trigger phase.
      Parameters:
      unit - The unit that currently possesses and is triggering this passive skill.

    • initCopy

      PassiveSkill initCopy()
      Safely creates and returns a fresh duplicate of this passive skill. Used as a secure alternative to Java's built-in clone() method to ensure deep copying of skill states when spawning new units from the deployment bag.
      Returns:
      A new, independent instance of the PassiveSkill.
